title: My Friends Come for Me, Not the Cushions
subtitle: An affirmation for the home that is good enough to share
excerpt: A short affirmation for the perfectionism that stops women from having people over until the house is finished, decorated, or in some better state. The home you have now is already a place where people can come.

practice.whenToUse: Use when you have declined an invitation or stopped yourself inviting someone because the house isn't ready, finished, or good enough.

content.text: A short affirmation for the habit of waiting until the house is better before sharing it. The rooms do not need to be finished. The cushions do not need to match. People come for the person who opens the door.
Say it aloud three times, at normal speaking speed. Once in front of a mirror if you can.
My friends come for me, not the cushions. My home is good enough to share today.

The affirmation has two parts. The first names what is actually true: nobody you want in your home is auditing the soft furnishings. The second is the practical conclusion: today is already a day you can have people over. Keep both sentences together. The second one needs the first to land.
